curation.synopsis: A hospital transport cast adrift in the warp carries the wounded of a hundred broken regiments — and a secret. As Horus's rebellion stretches the Imperium thin, the dead and dying are counted by the billion; greater still are those evacuated from the front to wait out the war in beds and stasis. On one such vessel a handful of half-conscious Imperial Army troopers piece together what has been happening to comrades who never returned, and discover that the missing have not simply died. A quiet, claustrophobic story about the human cost of the civil war, the men Malcador the Sigillite was already gathering in the dark, and the first foundations of a new kind of warrior the galaxy has not yet heard of.

flags.reason: named viewpoint troopers are not surfaced in accessible summaries; only Malcador's framing role is verifiable.
the derelict transport is not given a named registry or system in available sources; warp setting is plot-aspect, not a concrete location.
